channel.title: Web-Security on Robert Akhmerov | v31dt
channel.description: Recent content in Web-Security on Robert Akhmerov | v31dt

item.title: OAuth 2.1 as an Attack Surface
item.description:
OAuth is such a good attack surface because there is simply so much that can go wrong during implementation.

With OAuth 2.1 moving forward, and MCP authorization building on top of it, I think this space is going to get a lot of attention again.

The research angle is not only PKCE becoming mandatory or older flows being removed, but also the new client metadata / CIMD-style flows. When authorization servers start fetching client metadata URLs and resolving fields like logo_uri or jwks_uri, you get a fresh set of SSRF and trust-boundary issues to look at.

item.title: CVE-2026-25471: WordPress Admin Safety Guard Authentication Bypass
item.description:
Context

I discovered and responsibly disclosed CVE-2026-25471, a high-severity authentication bypass vulnerability in the WordPress Admin Safety Guard plugin by Themepaste. The issue affects vulnerable versions of the plugin up to the versions listed in the public advisory, and it was published by Patchstack in March 2026.

The vulnerable functionality was part of the plugin’s administrator login protection flow. That made the bug more interesting than a normal WordPress issue: the plugin was installed to make admin access safer, but the way it handled the OTP verification path created a separate route into the authentication process.
